content:
Here is a high-converting, markdown-formatted Dev.to post template designed to launch your text utility platform, engage developers, and build high-quality contextual backlinks.

🚀 Built a Free Suite of 10+ Online Text & AI Utilities for Developers (No Sign-ups, No Limits!)

Hey DEV community! 👋

As developers, writers, and power users, we manipulate text constantly. Whether it's stripping weird formatting, changing letter cases, formatting JSON, or trying to make sense of a dense 40-page contract, opening heavy software or dealing with ad-choked utility sites is a massive pain.

That’s why I built FreeToolForge — a completely free, fast, and lightweight hub packed with essential text utilities and AI helpers. No account required, no usage limits, just pure utility.

Here is a breakdown of what I built and the core tool I'm most excited to share with you all.

🔥 The Spotlight: AI Contract Simplifier

Reading through terms of service or enterprise agreements is exhausting. I built the AI Contract Simplifier to solve this.

You can paste dense legal agreements, and the AI instantly extracts:

• Parties Involved (Who is signing)

• Primary Obligations (What you actually have to do)

• Financial Terms (Fees, hidden costs)

• Key Deadlines & Renewal Conditions (So you don't get trapped)

It translates aggressive legal jargon into plain, actionable English. I would love for some of the developers here to stress-test it with complex open-source licenses or API terms!

🛠️ Other Essential Tools in the Forge

Aside from the AI helper, I’ve rolled out a suite of core text utilities that you can bookmark for your daily workflow:

• Case Converter: Instantly toggle text between camelCase, snake_case, PascalCase, UPPERCASE, and lowercase. Perfect for cleaning up variable lists.

• Find and Replace: Fast, browser-based regex and string swapping without opening an IDE.

• Word & Character Counter: Real-time analysis of your text length, reading time, and keyword density.

• Text Cleaners & Formatters: Strip unwanted whitespace, remove duplicate lines, and clean up messy data arrays instantly.

💡 Why Build This? (The Tech Stack)

Most online text tools are bloated, slow, and tracking-heavy. I wanted to focus on speed and privacy.

• The core text utilities run 100% client-side in your browser. Your data never touches a server.

• The layout is built to be clean, responsive, and distraction-free.

👇 I Need Your Feedback!

This is a living project, and I want to build what you actually use.

• What text tool or developer utility do you find yourself needing most often?

• Are there any specific formatting edge cases my tools missed?

Check out the full suite at FreeToolForge.org and let me know your thoughts, feature requests, or bugs in the comments below!

📈 Tips for Publishing This on Dev.to to Maximize Backlink Value:

• Use Exact URLs: When you post this, make sure to replace the general text-tools links above with the exact live URLs of those specific tools if they have their own dedicated sub-pages.

• Choose the Right Tags: Use tags like #devtools, #webdev, #opensource, and #ai. This pushes your post into the specific feeds of users looking for utilities.

• Engage in the Comments: When developers comment, reply immediately with a link to a specific tool that solves their problem. Dev.to comment sections pass great indexing signals to search engines.

Are there any other specific text tools you have live on the site right now that we should add to this post list?
